# coolify_hetzner_server_types (Data Source)

Lists all available Hetzner server types for a given cloud provider token.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "coolify_hetzner_server_types" "all" {
  cloud_provider_token_uuid = coolify_cloud_token.hetzner.uuid
}

output "hetzner_server_type_names" {
  value = [for st in data.coolify_hetzner_server_types.all.server_types : st.name]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `cloud_provider_token_uuid` (String) The UUID of the cloud provider token to use for listing Hetzner server types.

### Optional

- `filter` (Block Set) Filter results by field values. Multiple filters are ANDed together. Multiple values within a filter are ORed. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--filter))

### Read-Only

- `server_types` (Attributes List) The list of Hetzner server types. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--server_types))

<a id="nestedblock--filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `filter`

Required:

- `name` (String) The field name to filter by.
- `values` (Set of String) The values to match against. Multiple values are ORed.


<a id="nestedatt--server_types"></a>
### Nested Schema for `server_types`

Read-Only:

- `cores` (Number) The number of CPU cores.
- `description` (String) The description of the server type.
- `disk` (Number) The disk size in GB.
- `id` (Number) The numeric ID of the server type.
- `memory` (Number) The amount of memory in MB.
- `name` (String) The name of the server type.
